title: Allow using Verilated types in C++ `constexpr` contexts
body:
# Problem

SystemVerilog `localparam` arrays marked `/* verilator public */` are currently exposed as a `static constexpr` variable of type `VlUnpacked`.

```sv
localparam int foo[420]  /* verilator public */ = {420{1}};
```

```cpp
static constexpr VlUnpacked< /* something */ > foo = { /* a bunch of constants */ }; 
```

It is currently impossible to cleanly use `foo` in a `constexpr` context, such as:

```cpp
constexpr int bar(std::size_t index)
{
    return Vsomething::foo[index];
}

constexpr int baz = bar(1); // error
```

Instead, `bar` needs to be written as something like:

```cpp
constexpr int bar(std::size_t index)
{
    return Vsomething::foo.m_storage[index]; // Uses the internal "m_storage" which should be considered private
}
```

# Feature Request

Conditionally mark relevant functions in `verilated_types.h` as `constexpr` when C++14/C++17/C++20 is detected, allowing Verilated types to be used and accessed normally in a `constexpr` context.
code_blocks: static constexpr VlUnpacked< /* something */ > foo = { /* a bunch of constants */ };
